body:
I would propose to cluster all mail related configs into one object, e.g.:
```ts
mail: {
   options: ... // current mail options,
   defaultTemplate: ... // from line 24,
   contact: ... // new mails
}
```
btcInput
Do we need this address?

body: Name of the bank is Olkypay, not OlkyPay 😁  (everywhere)

I would propose to switch to a ref bonus of 0.1% instead. So the fee, when using a ref code, would be `crypto.fee - crypto.refBonus`.
For the buy fee we use a similar scheme, where the referred can adapt the ref bonus.
I would place this as an enum next to api-key.service.
These are 8 hours?
I would specify the time in seconds (as for JWT options)

This whole config doesn't really belong to blockchain, does it? I would move it somewhere else 😉 
(right now it's under `Config.blockchain.default...`
Update Bicep?
These are already under `transaction.minVolume`
This is already under `blockchain.default.minDeposit`
There is already a black squad config (bs) -> merge them
Maybe 1 single `Notifications` process?

body: I think I would remove the example settings and descriptions
I think I would remove the example settings and descriptions
What is the difference between `lnBitsApiUrl` and `lnBitsLnUrlPApiUrl`?
`lnUrlPApiUrl` is very hard to read, maybe handle LNURLP as one word, like `lnurlpApiUrl`? (same for `lnUrlPUrl`)
I think it would be good to do this for all variable names (e.g. `LnUrlPayRequestDto` -> `LnurlPayRequestDto`).
Remove start/end conditions `/[0-9A-Z]{4}-[0-9A-Z]{4}-[0-9A-Z]{4}/`
I wouldn't place HTTP config in the config file
Scan API config can be removed for Alchemy chains
Remove in Bicep (let's do it together 😉)
Visually separate new settings from old ones
